response.justification: Aisha brings 15+ years of broad full-stack experience with strong TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS, and Docker coverage — all core to this role. Her current position at re:cinq explicitly includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework work, which directly matches the AI agent development requirement. Her TDD background and testing library authorship signal testing maturity, though specific experience with Jest, Vitest, or Playwright is not mentioned. NestJS is absent from her history, and Kotlin/Java exposure appears incidental rather than substantive. Python is not listed anywhere. These gaps are meaningful but not disqualifying given her depth in the primary stack and demonstrated ability to ramp across technologies quickly.
strengths: Direct AI-agent infrastructure experience (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) at current role — rare and explicitly required
15+ years of full-stack breadth across TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS CDK, Docker, and Kubernetes
Strong testing culture: TDD advocate, created open-source D-language testing libraries, ran TDD workshops
concerns: NestJS not mentioned anywhere in employment history or skills — it is the primary backend framework for this role
Kotlin and Java appear only in peripheral contexts (robotics, mobile tooling); no production backend service ownership in JVM languages
Python absent from skills list entirely; no Vitest, Jest, or Playwright explicitly named despite testing being a core requirement
